fact: Tesla Supercharger cost estimate: ~$0.17/kWh vs Electrify America ~$0.50/kWh (~3x differential)
context: Energy cost advantage for Tesla-owned fleet charging at own Superchargers vs competitors using third-party networks

fact: Cybercab internal COGS estimate: ~$17,000–$18,000; consumer price: ~$25,000
context: Cern Basher's cost estimate — no competitor within 2-3x of Cybercab COGS

fact: Next closest competitor vehicle cost roughly double the Cybercab consumer price (~$50,000)
context: Competitive benchmark — even cheapest alternative is ~2x Cybercab price before sensor/compute upfit

fact: Vast majority of rides (80-90%+) involve 2 or fewer passengers, supporting Cybercab two-seat design
context: Validates Cybercab form factor — larger vehicles needed only for minority of rides

fact: Cybercab production expected to start in April 2026
context: Direct statement from Elon Musk confirming production start date for the dedicated robotaxi vehicle with no steering wheel or pedals

fact: Tesla expects to have fully autonomous vehicles operating in somewhere between a quarter and half of the United States by end of 2026, pending regulatory approval, and in dozens of major cities
context: Concrete geographic expansion target for 2026 unsupervised FSD deployment; federal preemption identified as a potential accelerant

fact: Unsupervised paid robotaxi rides launched in Austin with no safety monitor and no chase car as of approximately January 27, 2026
context: Key milestone: first commercial unsupervised FSD operation with paying customers and no human oversight in or following the vehicle

fact: FSD V14 shipped to customer vehicles, described as delivering a 'huge jump in performance'
context: Major FSD version release with significant capability upgrade observed by customers

fact: Optimus Gen 3 reveal expected in 'a few months' from January 2026
context: Confirms approximate reveal timeline for the next Optimus generation, described as looking human-like

fact: Model S and X production to wind down and stop next quarter (Q1 2026); Fremont factory space to be converted to Optimus production line with a target of 1 million units per year
context: Significant factory conversion announcement directly impacting Optimus production capacity and EV model lineup

fact: Tesla 2026 capex guidance is in excess of $20 billion, covering six new factories plus AI compute, existing factory expansion, and fleet expansion
context: Major step-up from 2025 capex (slightly below $9 billion guidance); signals scale of Tesla's investment phase in autonomy, robotics, and energy

fact: Tesla plans to build a 'Terafab' — a large-scale domestic semiconductor fab integrating logic, memory, and packaging — to address projected chip supply constraints in 3-4 years
context: New strategic initiative announced; not included in current $20B+ capex figure; seen as essential to avoid geopolitical supply risk and support Optimus/vehicle scaling

fact: Megapack 3 and Mega Block to launch in 2026
context: New product launches expected to drive increased energy deployments in 2026

fact: Robotaxi fleet carrying paid customers is well over 500 vehicles across Bay Area and Austin combined as of late January 2026
context: Current operational paid-ride fleet size; management indicated this would roughly double every month

fact: Cybercab expected to be used approximately 50 to 60 hours per week in autonomous operation, versus roughly 10 to 11 hours per week for a personally driven vehicle
context: Elon Musk's estimate of weekly utilization hours for Cybercab in robotaxi service; approximately 5x the utilization of a personal vehicle

fact: Personally driven vehicles are used approximately 1.5 hours per day on average, equating to roughly 10 hours per week
context: Baseline personal vehicle utilization figure used by Musk to contrast with expected Cybercab utilization

fact: Robotaxi fleet expected to roughly double every month
context: Elon Musk's qualitative growth rate projection for the robotaxi fleet; no specific annual number given, but monthly doubling implies rapid exponential ramp

fact: Significant Optimus production volume not expected until probably end of 2026
context: Timeline clarification for Optimus production ramp; current units are R&D phase only and not deployed materially in factories

fact: Tesla's AI chip roadmap includes AI5 (current focus) followed by AI6 aspirationally within under a year after AI5
context: Chip development timeline; AI5 completion described as Tesla's current top priority; both chips to be used in Tesla vehicles, Optimus, and data centers

fact: Tesla has chip supply agreements with TSMC in Arizona and Samsung in Texas; currently zero advanced memory fabs at scale exist in the United States
context: Highlights domestic supply chain gap for memory chips, motivating the proposed Terafab initiative

fact: Tesla solar cell production goal is 100 gigawatts per year, with full vertical integration from raw materials to finished panels
context: Major new strategic initiative in solar manufacturing; not yet included in the $20B+ capex figure; framed as critical for powering AI data centers

fact: Next-generation Roadster debut targeted for April 2026
context: New product reveal timeline for Tesla's flagship performance vehicle
